summary: A high‑energy show with Joan Baez guests and stellar Jerry work, captured on a modest‑quality recording that’s been cleaned up.
review: Reviewers rave about the excitement of Joan Baez joining the Dead for “Bobby McGee” and “It’s All Over Now, Baby Blue,” plus a ripping Big Boss Man and an unforgettable Shakedown Street opener. Jerry Garcia’s playing is repeatedly described as “fantastic” and “playing his brains out.” The tape isn’t pristine – listeners note it’s not the clearest but consider it clean enough after post‑processing.

ai_rating.rationale: Three enthusiastic 5‑star reviews praise the performance and deem the recording listenable after cleaning; only minor, consistent comments about clarity keep the rating just below perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Not the clearest source; reviewers mention some muddiness but also note that the cleaned‑up version sounds surprisingly clean, with overall acceptable balance for an audience‑type capture.

standout_songs: It’s All Over Now, Baby Blue
show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong opener (Shakedown) sets the tone; mid‑set features guest Joan Baez highlights; the show closes with an energetic jam reminiscent of early‑‘70 performances.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for being ‘stoked,’ delivering fantastic tone and playing his brains out.
